description: Tileset generation pipeline configuration. Each style is a master prompt that governs a full generation run. Runs are logged with results.

styles.name: 16px Retro
styles.prompt: Create a 16x16 pixel art tile for a medieval fantasy strategy game. Style: classic retro pixel art with a limited 32-color palette, reminiscent of early DOS/NES games. Hard pixel edges, no anti-aliasing. Consistent lighting from top-left. Dark outlines on sprites. Each tile must read clearly at 16x16.
styles.palette_notes: Limited palette, earthy tones for terrain, bright accents for units/buildings. No gradients.

styles.name: 32px SNES Era
styles.prompt: Create a 32x32 pixel art tile for a medieval fantasy strategy game. Style: detailed retro pixel art, SNES/GBA era quality. Rich but controlled palette (~64 colors). Subtle shading with 2-3 shade levels per color. Consistent top-left lighting. Tiles should seamlessly connect to adjacent tiles of the same type. Slight dithering for texture on terrain.
styles.palette_notes: Rich earthy palette. Greens for vegetation, blues for water, warm browns for buildings. Gold accents for important structures.

styles.name: 64px Modern Pixel
styles.prompt: Create a 64x64 pixel art tile for a medieval fantasy strategy game. Style: modern detailed pixel art with rich color depth. Smooth shading gradients, detailed textures. Top-down orthographic view. Consistent warm lighting from top-left. Anti-aliased edges where appropriate. Each tile should have fine detail visible at this resolution.
styles.palette_notes: Full color range. Realistic but stylized. Ambient occlusion on buildings. Texture detail on terrain.

styles.name: 32px Painted Miniature
styles.prompt: Create a 32x32 painted miniature style tile for a medieval fantasy strategy game. Style: hand-painted tabletop miniature feel, like a Warhammer or D&D battle map. Soft brush strokes visible, watercolor-like blending. Warm parchment undertones. Consistent top-left lighting with soft shadows. Should feel like looking at a painted game board.
styles.palette_notes: Watercolor palette. Muted earth tones with occasional vivid accents. Parchment/cream base tone showing through.

styles.name: 64px Isometric
styles.prompt: Create a 64x64 isometric tile for a medieval fantasy strategy game. Style: isometric 3/4 top-down view, like classic Civilization or Age of Empires. The tile diamond is oriented with corners pointing up/down/left/right. Clean edges for seamless isometric tiling. Consistent lighting from top-left. Elevation should be visually apparent in the tile height.
styles.palette_notes: Vibrant but grounded. Terrain has depth through isometric perspective. Buildings show rooftops and facades.

generation_order.description: Order in which tiles are generated within a run. First tiles establish the style that later tiles reference.

phases.name: anchors
phases.description: Generate style-defining tiles first. These set the visual baseline.
phases.reference_strategy: none — these ARE the style anchors

phases.name: terrain_complete
phases.description: Remaining terrain and vegetation tiles, referencing phase 1 anchors.
phases.reference_strategy: include phase 1 anchors as style reference

phases.name: buildings
phases.description: All designation tiles. Reference terrain tiles for visual consistency.
phases.reference_strategy: include flat+good and flat+forest as background context

phases.name: units
phases.description: Army and navy tiles. Reference terrain for scale consistency.
phases.reference_strategy: include flat+good as background, show unit AT SCALE on terrain

phases.name: race_variants
phases.description: Race-specific unit variants. Each references the base army tile + race aesthetic.
phases.reference_strategy: include base army tile + race description as style modifier

reference_strategies.description: Different approaches to maintaining visual consistency across a generation run.

strategies.name: Progressive Refinement
strategies.description: Each new tile includes the PREVIOUS tile as a style reference. Chain: tile1 → tile2(ref:tile1) → tile3(ref:tile2) → etc. Style drifts but stays locally consistent.

strategies.name: Anchor Reference
strategies.description: All tiles reference the same 3-5 anchor tiles from phase 1. Most consistent overall style.

strategies.name: Category Anchor
strategies.description: Each category (terrain, vegetation, buildings, units) has its own anchor tile. Within-category consistency is high, cross-category may vary slightly.
strategies.best_for: When categories need distinct but harmonious styles

strategies.name: Cumulative Gallery
strategies.description: Each new tile references ALL previously generated tiles (or a grid/collage of them). Most context for the model but may hit token/size limits.
strategies.best_for: Small tilesets where you can fit the gallery in context

strategies.name: Race Grouped
strategies.description: Generate all tiles for one race together, using that race's aesthetic as the anchor. Then next race. Cross-race variation is intentional.

curation.description: Workflow for iterating toward a complete tileset the user likes.

workflow.description: Generate all tiles for a style. Log everything.
User reviews each tile. Marks as KEEP, REGENERATE, or SKIP.
Kept tiles are locked. They won't be regenerated and WILL be used as style references for regeneration.
Regenerate only the tiles marked REGENERATE. Use locked/kept tiles as style references so new tiles match the approved ones.
Back to step 2. Repeat until all tiles are KEEP or user accepts the set.

storage.description: Where tiles and run data live
